title: feat: allow serializing closures into records
body:
This allows closures to be serialized into records using:

```nu
{ some closure code } | into record
```

and deserialized and ran with

```nu
[closure record] | into closure | do $in
```

This is useful when transferring closures over an Internet connection, such as when implementing a tool similar to ssh. Sending a closure instead of just sending code as a string allows users to easily capture variables, other closures, etc to send to a remote server for a particular computation. Encoding the closure as a record allows it to be encoded in a format like json to be sent over a network.

## Release notes summary - What our users need to know
Closures can be serialized using `into record` and and deserialized using `into closure`

## Tasks after submitting
- [ ] Update the [documentation](https://github.com/nushell/nushell.github.io)
